source_pr.title: planner: use collected predicate columns to do stats sync load
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #56812

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Just as the issue said, we can directly use predicate columns that we collect to decide the statistics' loading.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Improve the performance of statistics' sync loading
优化统计信息同步加载的性能
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared derived partition/column lists whose order depended on map/slice iteration order, so results could differ between runs even when logically equivalent. This PR stabilizes it by sorting the derived partition name lists (and related collections) before asserting.
fix_pattern: Sort derived lists (e.g., expanded partition names) before assertions to avoid relying on map iteration order.

evidence: files: pkg/executor/test/analyzetest/analyze_test.go, pkg/planner/core/casetest/planstats/BUILD.bazel, pkg/planner/core/casetest/planstats/plan_stats_test.go, pkg/planner/core/casetest/planstats/testdata/plan_stats_suite_in.json, pkg/planner/core/casetest/planstats/testdata/plan_stats_suite_out.json, pkg/planner/core/collect_column_stats_usage.go, pkg/planner/core/collect_column_stats_usage_test.go, pkg/planner/core/rule_collect_plan_stats.go, pkg/planner/planctx/context.go, pkg/session/session.go, pkg/sessionctx/context.go, pkg/statistics/handle/storage/read.go, pkg/statistics/handle/syncload/stats_syncload_test.go, pkg/statistics/handle/usage/BUILD.bazel, pkg/statistics/handle/usage/session_stats_collect.go, pkg/statistics/table.go, pkg/util/mock/context.go
